mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when industrial production rises during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.3% trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in over 65% of months following inflation spikes above 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict positive 60-day returns in a majority of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDP growth surprises over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity that immediately reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks fail to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.
